skill.name: ui-handler
skill.description: Guides UI implementation with Shadcn MCP for atoms, 21st.dev MCP for larger sections, and local styling conventions.
skill.summary: Build consistent app interfaces with Shadcn components, 21st.dev sections, and project-level styling guidance.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and high-risk matches, but most are false positives from markdown inline code, paths, or ordinary UI text. One real concern remains: the skill recommends a package-manager fallback command that can execute external tooling and modify project files.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Command Fallback
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md recommends `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add {component}` as a manual fallback. This is legitimate UI tooling, but it can execute code from an external package source and should require normal package review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present and would invoke external package tooling. The surrounding context is normal component installation, so the concern is supply-chain and command execution risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Tokens Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: Most static external-command matches are inline markdown references to paths, component imports, CSS utilities, and MCP names. They are documentation tokens, not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is visible as markdown prose and inline code. No executable Ruby, shell script, interpolation, or runtime code path is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal and Weak Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The path traversal alert maps to `src/app/.../_components/`, which is an illustrative path pattern. The weak cryptography alerts do not show cryptographic operations or algorithm use at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain UI guidance, responsive design wording, or placeholder path notation. I found no evidence of file traversal logic, hashing, encryption, or weak cryptographic algorithm selection.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The fallback command uses `pnpm dlx` with `shadcn@latest`, which can download and execute package code during component installation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicit and uses a package execution workflow. The context is legitimate, but the pattern is still sensitive because it depends on external package integrity.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent UI work without rebuilding common components from scratch. This skill directs Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Shadcn, 21st.dev, Tailwind, and local project conventions.
actual_capabilities: Directs agents to add Shadcn atoms such as buttons, inputs, and dialogs.
Guides use of 21st.dev Magic MCP for hero sections, dashboards, and larger blocks.
Defines preferred component locations for shared UI, sections, and page-specific files.
Recommends Tailwind utility classes and semantic theme variables for styling.
Documents dark mode and responsive design expectations for generated UI.
Provides a fallback Shadcn command when MCP component installation is unavailable.
limitations: It does not include component source code or templates by itself.
It depends on Shadcn MCP, 21st.dev MCP, or local package tooling being available.
Generated UI quality depends on the prompt and the existing project structure.
The fallback command can change dependencies and should be reviewed before use.

use_cases.title: Add Standard UI Controls
use_cases.description: Install and integrate buttons, inputs, dialogs, and other reusable atoms using established Shadcn patterns.

use_cases.title: Create Product Page Sections
use_cases.description: Generate hero areas, footers, and dashboard sections with 21st.dev while keeping files in expected locations.

use_cases.title: Keep Styling Consistent
use_cases.description: Update themes, CSS variables, responsive behavior, and dark mode support using the project styling rules.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Components
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ui-handler skill to add a Shadcn button and dialog to this app. Follow the existing component locations.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Responsive Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ui-handler skill to generate a responsive SaaS hero section with 21st.dev MCP and integrate it into the current page.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Theme Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ui-handler skill to update the app theme with semantic CSS variables and ensure dark mode remains supported.

prompt_templates.title: Compose a Full Page Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ui-handler skill to plan and implement a page with header, sidebar, content area, shared atoms, and page-specific components.

output_examples.input: Add a settings dialog to the account page.
output_examples.output: The agent adds the Shadcn dialog dependency, places reusable primitives in the shared UI folder, and wires the dialog into the account page.

output_examples.input: Create a new marketing hero for a SaaS product.
output_examples.output: The agent prompts 21st.dev for a responsive hero, saves the section in the sections folder, and imports it into the page.

output_examples.input: Make the dashboard respect dark mode.
output_examples.output: The agent updates styles to use semantic theme variables instead of fixed colors and checks responsive behavior.

best_practices: Review any package-manager command before running it in a production repository.
Use shared UI folders for reusable atoms and page folders for unique components.
Prefer semantic Tailwind classes and CSS variables over fixed color values.
anti_patterns: Do not generate large custom components before checking Shadcn or 21st.dev options.
Do not place reusable components inside a single page folder.
Do not hardcode light mode colors when semantic theme variables exist.

faq.question: Does this skill create UI components directly?
faq.answer: It guides an agent to use Shadcn MCP, 21st.dev MCP, or local commands to create and place components.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it can recommend a package-manager command.

faq.question: Why is the audit not marked safe?
faq.answer: The fallback command can download and execute external package code, which needs normal supply-chain review.

faq.question: Does it include network calls or data exfiltration?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the reviewed files for network calls, credential access, or data exfiltration.

faq.question: Can it help with dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference guidance recommends semantic variables so components support dark mode automatically.
